hero.title: Access Michigan Services, Simplified
hero.subtitle: One structured gateway to health, housing, food, and family services across all 83 counties.

access.sectionSubtitle: Three pathways to the services Michigan residents need most.
access.housingDesc: Emergency shelter, rental assistance, utility programs, and pathways to stable housing across Michigan.
access.foodDesc: SNAP enrollment, food banks, WIC, prescription assistance, and financial aid programs statewide.
access.healthDesc: Find doctors, clinics, mental health support, prenatal care, and insurance enrollment options nearby.

systems.title: What Is Michigan Access?
systems.description: Michigan Access is an independent, non-commercial resource that organizes public service information from across state agencies into a single, structured interface. It does not replace government services — it makes them easier to find, understand, and act on.

prototype.title: Portfolio Prototype:
prototype.description: Demonstrating data integration methodology using 2024–2025 public datasets.

quickActions.findCareDesc: Search doctors, hospitals, urgent care by location and specialty
quickActions.transportationDesc: Public transit, school buses, automated enforcement, accessible rides for seniors
quickActions.environmentDesc: Air quality, water safety, clean energy, Great Lakes conservation, recycling
quickActions.civicDataDesc: State budget, FOIA requests, voter info, elected officials, public meetings
quickActions.financialHelpDesc: Free and reduced-cost care, insurance enrollment, prescription assistance
quickActions.conditionsDesc: Evidence-based health information, treatment options, care pathways
quickActions.communityResourcesDesc: Food, housing, transportation, support groups, social services

stats.updatedWeekly: Updated weekly with latest health data

howItHelps.title: How This Platform Helps You
howItHelps.subtitle: Built for Michigan families, by someone who cares
howItHelps.findQualityCareDesc: Compare healthcare options using independent quality ratings, patient safety scores, and service availability.
howItHelps.navigateSystemsDesc: Understand insurance options, find financial assistance, access social services.
howItHelps.stayInformedDesc: Track Michigan health trends, learn about conditions, discover prevention strategies.

featuredTopics.title: Featured Health Topics
featuredTopics.subtitle: Stay informed about what matters most to Michigan families
featuredTopics.fluTitle: Flu Season: Where to Get Vaccinated
featuredTopics.fluDesc: Find vaccination sites near you. Walk-in and appointment options available across all 83 counties.
featuredTopics.heartDesc: Michigan's #1 cause of death. Learn about prevention strategies and find top-rated cardiac care.
featuredTopics.mentalDesc: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline now active. New community mental health centers opening statewide.
featuredTopics.maternalDesc: Michigan sees 12% improvement in maternal outcomes. New programs expanding access to prenatal care.

trust.dataFrom: Data from CMS, HRSA, CDC, Michigan DHHS
trust.privacyFirst: No Cookies · No Personal Data Collection

footer.disclaimer: This platform is an independent, non-commercial civic resource. It does not collect personal data, use cookies for tracking, display advertising, or sell information.
footer.noAds: No advertising · No data selling · Built to serve Michigan residents
footer.ethicalNote: This project is built with an ethical, non-commercial ethos — prioritizing public benefit over profit.
footer.dataSourceNote: Some metrics may reflect a 3–12 month lag from source agencies.
footer.brandDescription: An independent civic resource organizing Michigan's public services into one structured, accessible interface.
footer.privacyStatement: No cookies, no tracking, no personal data collected.
footer.dataAttribution: Data: CMS · HRSA · CDC · Michigan DHHS · Leapfrog

partnership.title: Partnership Portal
partnership.subtitle: Non-profits, community organizations, and service providers can submit resources for inclusion on Michigan Access.
partnership.success: Your submission has been received and will be reviewed by our team.

findCare.title: Find Care Near You
findCare.subtitle: Search Michigan healthcare facilities by location, specialty, quality ratings, and services.
findCare.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, city, county, or specialty...
findCare.noResultsHint: Try adjusting your search or filters

healthMap.title: Health Map
healthMap.description: Explore healthcare facilities across Michigan. Toggle layers to show different facility types. Markers are color-coded by quality score.
healthMap.dataNoticeText: Facility names and locations are real Michigan healthcare facilities sourced from CMS Hospital Compare. Quality scores reflect available public data from CMS, Leapfrog Group, ANCC Magnet Recognition, and BCBSM Blue Distinction. Some metrics may have a 3–12 month lag from source agencies.
healthMap.demoScores: Scores are demo data. Real data from CMS, Leapfrog, ANCC.

environment.title: Michigan's Environmental Health
environment.subtitle: Air quality, water safety, clean energy progress, recycling programs, and environmental justice — data-driven insights for Michigan's natural resources.
environment.waterViolationsDesc: Public water system violations across Michigan (2019–2024) · Source: Michigan EGLE
environment.renewableGrowthDesc: Percentage of total energy generation from renewable sources (2018–2024) · Source: U.S. EIA
environment.advisoriesDesc: Check your local utility for the latest boil-water and do-not-drink advisories currently in effect across Michigan communities.
environment.improvingTrendDesc: Violations decreased 50% since 2019 with ongoing Flint, Benton Harbor, and statewide infrastructure investments.

civic.title: Michigan Open Government Hub
civic.subtitle: Budget transparency, FOIA tracking, voter engagement, elected officials, and public meeting schedules — empowering informed civic participation.
civic.foiaVolumeDesc: Michigan Freedom of Information Act requests to state agencies (2019–2024)
civic.voterTurnoutChart: Michigan Voter Turnout vs. National Average
civic.voterTurnoutDesc: Percentage of eligible voters who cast ballots (2016–2024)
civic.registerToVoteDesc: Register online, by mail, or in person — including same-day registration at your local clerk's office.
civic.findPollingPlaceDesc: Look up your polling location, sample ballot, and elected officials by address.
civic.trackBallotDesc: Check the status of your absentee ballot from application to counting.
civic.howToFOIA: How to File a FOIA Request in Michigan

financial.title: Help Paying for Healthcare
financial.subtitle: Find free and reduced-cost care, insurance options, prescription savings, and social services available to Michigan residents.
financial.eligibilityDesc: See which programs you may qualify for based on household size and income
financial.youMayQualifyFor: you may qualify for based on
financial.medicaidQualify: You likely qualify for Medicaid/Healthy Michigan Plan.
financial.marketplaceQualify: You may qualify for marketplace subsidies and charity care programs.

conditions.title: Health Condition Guides
conditions.subtitle: Find the best care for your condition — facilities, specialists, and support services ranked by quality and accessibility.
conditions.diabetesDesc: Find endocrinologists, diabetes education, nutrition counseling, and community support near you.
conditions.cancerDesc: Access oncology centers, treatment facilities, support groups, and financial assistance for cancer care.
conditions.pregnancyDesc: Find OB/GYN providers, birthing centers, prenatal care, and postpartum support services.
conditions.noFacilities: No matching facilities found for this pathway.
conditions.needHelp: Need help finding the right care?
conditions.needHelpDesc: Our resources connect you to quality care, financial help, and community support.

resources.title: Community Resources
resources.subtitle: Health goes beyond the doctor's office. Find food, housing, transportation, and mental health support near you.
resources.noResourcesHint: Try adjusting your filters or search
resources.crisisBanner: In a crisis? Call or text 988 for the Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
